scores.materiality_reasoning: Conducting worship is a social/priesthood role with symbolic payoff, though it yields some social status.
scores.generality_reasoning: A monopoly on divine communication spans all religious life for the group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Occupational priesthood/Magi status is described as a lifelong structural position.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The Magi are presented as a hereditary or strictly closed religious/ethnic caste.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Diogenes reports on the custom of ancient Persian Magi from a historically distant perspective.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim reports the exclusivity of their role as an observed ethnographic fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a Magus does not inherently define one's monopoly on 'the ear' of gods in all cultures.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Personal ornaments and gold are tangible property and physical wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to luxury goods and clothing style, a recurring attribute of the group.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Part of a lifelong ascetic commitment inherent to the group's structural role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The Magi are a structural hereditary/religious caste, hard to enter or leave.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports on ancient Persian customs centuries before the author's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Direct description of observed ascetic prohibitions and dress codes.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Religious membership does not conceptually require a ban on wearing gold.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Food is a concrete material resource essential for subsistence.
scores.generality_reasoning: Restricts a specific category of life (diet) across many recurring daily scenarios.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong dietary restrictions associated with the ascetic life of the Magi.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Part of a structural caste-like religious identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports ancient/foreign customs of the Persian Magi.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of the actual food types consumed by the group.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Religious status does not analytically imply eating only cheese and vegetables.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Disposal of the body (cremation) is a tangible physical treatment of human remains.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a single specific procedure (funerary rites).
scores.persistence_reasoning: A rule governing the finality of death, applying to every group member's end of life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Magi as a structural hereditary or dedicated life-long religious group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Diogenes cites historical accounts (Sotion) regarding ancient Persian customs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported fact involving their moral 'deeming' of the practice as impious.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Magian does not define cremation as impious; that is a specific theological rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Intellectual recognition/status as 'inventors' is abstract and non-material recognition.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of intellectual domains (geometry, astronomy, etc.).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Historical legacy/claim is a permanent structural feature of the ethnic group's identity.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ethnicity (Egyptian) is biologically and birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the origins of sciences in the deep, legendary past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports on the Egyptians' own claims ('they claimed to have invented').
scores.tautology_reasoning: National identity does not logically entail being the creator of mathematics.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The title of 'Sage' is a purely symbolic, reputational honor.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a very specific, elite, and selective honorific title.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Historical naming among the 'Seven Sages' is a permanent structural legacy.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility is defined here by descent from Cadmus/Agenor (lineage).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the archaic period (Archonship of Damasias).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites various historians regarding Thales' lineage and receipt of the name.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being noble does not automatically mean one is a Sage.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Political participation allows direct influence over state material resources and war.
scores.generality_reasoning: Politics is a foundational societal axis covering many outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship rights are long-term (lifelong) but legally revocable.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal status difficult to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Thales and Croesus (6th century BCE).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports Thales' political activities as a matter of historical record.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship is the prerequisite for political advice, not the definition of it.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Industrial equipment (oil-mills) and amassing fortunes are tangible material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to specific commercial monopolies/leasing is a recurring specific economic right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Wealth and capital leverage act as long-term advantages in a person's life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is changeable with effort (as the story explicitly proves).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Anecdote about the pre-Socratic philosopher Thales.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported anecdote from Hieronymus of Rhodes as historical fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Having wealth is the means to monopoly, but monopoly is not the definition of wealth.

scores.materiality_reasoning: 'Blessings' and 'fortune' in this context are abstract framing of life quality.
scores.generality_reasoning: Being male vs. female is a foundational axis for all life outcomes in this society.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Sex/gender is a lifelong structural characteristic.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Sex is biologically fixed at birth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Anecdote attributed to Socrates/Thales in the past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is a rhetorical expression of gratitude/opinion by the speaker.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman is not analytically defined by having less 'blessing'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: 'Blessing' is an abstract, non-material categorization of status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Greek vs. Barbarian is a foundational societal/legal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ethnicity/origin is a lifelong, permanent status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being Greek vs. Barbarian is defined by birth/lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical anecdote of pre-Socratic/Socratic era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Rhetorical expression of the speaker's personal valuation/prejudice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Greek is not the definition of having good fortune.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Judicial/legal dispute resolution has direct effects on property (fish, tripods).
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to resolve disputes is a specific recurring legal procedure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The colonial relationship and subordination of justice hold while the colony exists.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Residency in a colony (Lebedus) is a durable identity but changeable through relocation.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical account of the archaic relations between Miletus and its colonies.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Narrative of an actual historical diplomatic conflict and legal custom.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a colonist doesn't conceptually mean you cannot settle fish disputes locally.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from slavery and retention of property are foundational material protections.
scores.generality_reasoning: Freedom vs. enslavement is a foundational structural axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Freedom from debt-slavery is a lifelong legal standing for the citizen body.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses Solon's laws (6th century BCE) from a historical distance.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the 'Law of Release' as a specific, well-known historical statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not naturally include immunity from debt slavery without this law.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Proposing war affects state resources, lives, and individual political standing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a single hyper-specific policy scenario (the Salaminian war).
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-time decree governing a specific historical conflict.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is hard to change and legally fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the archaic Athenian legal response to war with Megara.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim reports a specific legal decree as a historical event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship usually allows speech; silencing them on war is a specific rule.

scores.materiality_reasoning: A 'tithe' is a direct 10% reduction in tangible property/wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Taxation is a broad category of state-citizen material relation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Tax obligations are a long-term/recurring state of being a property-owning citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is structural and difficult to change.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the tyranny of Pisistratus in the 6th century BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The letter reports the tax as a plain factual obligation of citizenship.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Paying 10% is not the definition of being a citizen.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Disfranchisement (loss of rights) is a legal status removal with major material effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political rights cover a broad range of life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of citizenship rights is typically a permanent or long-term structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a 'child' (offspring) of parents is an immutable biological fact of lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites the historical laws of Solon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific statutes regarding parental care and spendthrifts.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a child does not define one as being a provider for parents.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participating in an occupation has material outcomes, but here it's more about the role requirement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the broad requirement for economic activity/labor across all citizens.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The requirement for a 'settled occupation' applies across a person's entire adult life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The 'anyone' (citizens) is a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses Solonian/draconian laws from centuries past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the existence of a specific law and the right of impeachment for it.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not conceptually require having a 'settled occupation'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Speaking in the Assembly is a political/social practice with material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the recurring procedure of speaking in the sovereign body.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The deprivation of rights is a long-term or permanent legal state once enacted.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Refers to Citizens, a stable structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reports laws attributed to Draco and Solon.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual reporting of a statute as cited by Lysias.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship and morality (not being profligate) are not analytically linked.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Restricting marriage has direct effects on kinship, inheritance, and social status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to marry certain people is a specific legal/social boundary.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Guardianship is a long-term role during a ward's minority.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a 'Guardian' is a role-bound legal appointment, not an immutable trait.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites historical Solonian legislation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the terms of a specific and 'excellent' historical provision.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The role of guardian does not inherently include marital restrictions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Retaining a physical impression/copy of a product is a tangible property right.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific professional technicality regarding ring impressions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Governs a momentary transaction (the sale of one specific ring).
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation (engraver) is a durable identity acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Historical Athenian professional regulation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific legal prohibition on professional conduct.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an engraver does not conceptually require hand over of impressions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Execution (the death penalty) is the ultimate physical/bodily deprivation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific: limited to the scenario of intoxication while in office.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A one-time penalty for a single specific episode of conduct.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Magistrate is a temporary role-bound office during a term.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a historical Solonian law from ancient Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Succinct reporting of a specific historical statute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of a magistrate does not involve the threat of execution for drinking.
